articles.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
articles.summary: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

articles.title: Iran-backed hackers breach FBI director Kash Patel's personal emails
articles.summary: A hacker group shares Patel's purported resume and photos of him on its website along with a statement that says, "This is just our beginning."

articles.title: Spanish woman who died through euthanasia failed by state, say critics
articles.summary: Noelia Castillo died on Thursday evening in a Barcelona hospital, after a protracted legal battle with her own father.

articles.title: Austria becomes latest to propose social media ban for children
articles.summary: The proposed under-14 ban follows similar steps in other European countries, including France and Spain.

articles.title: Rubio says US expects to finish Iran war 'in next couple of weeks'
articles.summary: Marco Rubio said US allies were open to helping escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has closed and threatened to charge fees for.

articles.title: Two Cuba-bound aid ships missing after leaving Mexico
articles.summary: Mexico's navy launches a rescue mission for ships delivering aid to Cuba, strained by food and fuel shortages amid a US embargo.

articles.title: Savannah Guthrie to return to Today show as search for mother continues
articles.summary: The US television presenter will return to hosting the NBC news programme in April.

articles.title: Three dead after helicopter crash in Hawaii
articles.summary: The helicopter, which was carrying one pilot and four passengers,crash-landed about 100 yards off a beach, officials say.

articles.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
articles.summary: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.
